studyName: Single-Cell Atlas of Human Liver and Blood Immune Cells Across Fatty Liver Disease Stages

variables.name: AGE
variables.description: Age of the subject when the sample was collected

variables.name: ALBUMIN
variables.description: A protein made by the liver; low levels may indicate liver dysfunction

variables.name: ALP
variables.description: Alkaline Phosphatase, an enzyme related to liver and bone health

variables.name: ALT
variables.description: Alanine Aminotransferase, an enzyme elevated in liver injury

variables.name: APRI_SCORE
variables.description: Index that estimates liver fibrosis using AST levels and platelet count

variables.name: AST
variables.description: Aspartate Aminotransferase, an enzyme used to assess liver damage

variables.name: AST ULN
variables.description: Upper Limit of Normal for AST, used to assess whether AST is elevated

variables.name: BMI
variables.description: Body Mass Index, a measure of body fat based on height and weight

variables.name: CAP
variables.description: Controlled Attenuation Parameter, quantifying liver fat via ultrasound

variables.name: CD163
variables.description: Soluble receptor from macrophages, often elevated in liver inflammation

variables.name: CLINICAL_STAGE
variables.description: Stage of progression for MASLD/MASH (Metabolic Dysfunction-associated Steatotic Liver Disease); F0-F4 refer to Brunt fibrosis stages

variables.name: ETHNICITY
variables.description: The ethnic background of the subject

variables.name: FIB4_SCORE
variables.description: Calculation estimating liver fibrosis based on age, AST, ALT, and platelets

variables.name: FIBROSCAN
variables.description: Non-invasive test measuring liver stiffness to assess fibrosis

variables.name: FIBROSIS_STAGE
variables.description: Stage of liver fibrosis, indicating the degree of liver scarring

variables.name: GLOBULIN
variables.description: Group of proteins in the blood; levels may be altered in liver disease

variables.name: GLYCOGENATED_NUCLEI
variables.description: Presence of glycogen accumulation in liver cell nuclei, a histological feature

variables.name: HEPATOCYTE_BALLOONING
variables.description: Swelling of liver cells (hepatocytes), a sign of liver damage

variables.name: INR
variables.description: International Normalized Ratio, measuring blood clotting ability

variables.name: LOBULAR_INFLAMMATION
variables.description: Inflammation in the liver's lobules, indicative of liver injury

variables.name: NAFLD_ACTIVITY_SCORE
variables.description: Composite score evaluating the severity of NAFLD based on histology

variables.name: PLT
variables.description: Platelet count in the blood, important for assessing liver function

variables.name: PORTAL_INFLAMMATION
variables.description: Inflammation in the liver's portal areas, a marker of chronic liver disease

variables.name: RACE
variables.description: The racial background of the subject

variables.name: SEX
variables.description: Biological sex of the subject (male or female)

variables.name: STEATOHEPATITIS
variables.description: Liver inflammation associated with fat accumulation, often leading to nonalcoholic steatohepatitis (NASH)

variables.name: STEATOSIS_GRADE
variables.description: Grading of liver fat accumulation (steatosis) severity

variables.name: SUBJECT_ID
variables.description: Unique identifier for the subject

variables.name: TOTAL_BILI
variables.description: Total bilirubin levels, indicating liver function and bile processing

variables.name: TOTAL_PROTEIN
variables.description: Total protein in the blood, reflecting liver function and nutritional status

variables.name: ANALYTE_TYPE
variables.description: The analyte type of the sample (ex. DNA, RNA)

variables.name: BODY_SITE
variables.description: The collection site of the sample

variables.name: IS_TUMOR
variables.description: The tumor status of the sample

variables.name: SAMPLE_ID
variables.description: Sample ID

variables.name: CONSENT
variables.description: Registered consent groups (Data Use Limitations (DUL)) as determined by submitters' Institutional Review Boards (IRB) or equivalent body

variables.name: SEX
variables.description: Biological sex

variables.name: SUBJECT_ID
variables.description: Subject ID

variables.name: SAMPLE_ID
variables.description: Sample ID

variables.name: SUBJECT_ID
variables.description: Subject ID
